programmes.show_description: Quisps: David Baddiel shows a readiness to bare his chest, Katy Wix robs a piggy bank, and Alex is powerless in the face of Ed Gamble's creative vision.
Dara O Briain spins the Wheel of News once more as Gary Delaney, Rob Beckett, Romesh Ranganathan and Josh Widdicombe join Hugh and Andy to wittily dissect the week's headlines.
Famous fibbers Greg Rutherford, Kirsty Young, Joanna Scanlan and Henning Wehn deride their fellow guests' truth-handling abilities in the deceitful quiz hosted by Rob Brydon.
The Best Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return in an episode packed with some of the best bits from Series 14, with guests including Richard Osman and Samantha Morton.
Sovereign Citizens: Jamali finds and follows those who have gained the FBI classification of domestic terrorists by refusing to recognise the US Government, its laws and its currency.
Medieval and Macabre: Enlightenment on the Middle Ages with Stephen Fry. Julia Zemiro, Matt Lucas, David Mitchell and Alan Davies hear that pre-fabs date to pre-Ikea times!
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Island Life: The biker boys continue their countrywide hunt for great grub. They celebrate our love of fish and shellfish and find out about Billingsgate Market.
A family from Venezuela plead with officers to enter America. In Progreso, a suspected drug dealer is caught smuggling pills in his underpants.
More than 100 boxes full of drugs are discovered inside an unlikely vehicle at the World Trade Bridge. In Progreso, a suspected gang member attempts to cross into the US.
Documentary series. Cracks appear in a multi-billion-dollar iron ore railroad, and learner driver James must survive the punishing Cockatoo run and the ire of instructor Dave Boyd.
Sri Lanka and Bali: Rick Stein's culinary odyssey continues. In Sri Lanka, he discovers a wealth of hot and spicy vegetable curries, and in Bali he samples some amazing roast pork.
Meth is found inside a truck driver's speakers, a Mexican boy claiming US citizenship is questioned, and AC units containing drugs are intercepted.
A large shipment of fake fentanyl pills is intercepted, an automatic rifle is found inside a loudspeaker, and a woman dealing drugs for her boyfriend is captured.
Firefighter: Guy follows in the footsteps of his brother and sister when he trains to become a firefighter in some scarily real environments at a fire station in Grimsby.
Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz are the Hollywood Stars in a Reasonably Priced Car, while Captain Slow quickens the pace as he tries to break his personal best in a Bugatti Veyron.
Jeremy, Richard and James take a nostalgic trip through the UK's sports car industry in some old British roadsters from Jensen-Healey, Lotus and TVR. Actor Jeff Goldblum guests.
Island Life: The biker boys continue their countrywide hunt for great grub. They celebrate our love of fish and shellfish and find out about Billingsgate Market.
Sri Lanka and Bali: Rick Stein's culinary odyssey continues. In Sri Lanka, he discovers a wealth of hot and spicy vegetable curries, and in Bali he samples some amazing roast pork.
Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return with an episode of previously unseen material from the fourteenth series of the show.
Retired footballer David James, comedians Rhys James and Isy Suttie, and athletics legend Denise Lewis tackle the best-selling author's fun-filled brainteasers in this edition.
More mind-bending puzzles and fun with the ever-genial Pointless personality - he's joined this time by Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams.
Monster Mash: The smash-hit quiz show that caught on in a flash. Beastly quizmaster Stephen Fry is an eerie sight for Sara Pascoe, Josh Widdicombe, Phill Jupitus and Alan Davies.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Professor Hannah Fry is host as comedian Zoe Lyons and doctor and comedian Ed Patrick join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Another Spoon: Jo Brand shows cowboys how to cowboy, Katy Wix tortures Alex Horne with the help of a garden arch, and Rose Matafeo asks a stranger if they have a library card.
A Tory conference gets a pasting in the rapid-fire news quiz, and Milton Jones, Seann Walsh, Miles Jupp and Josh Widdicombe join the regulars to suggest 'Unlikely Small Ads'!
Panel show giggles. Jimmy Carr, Griff Rhys Jones, Dave Myers and Susanna Reid join Rob Brydon, David Mitchell and Lee Mack to discern unlikely fact from plausible fiction.
Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return with an episode of previously unseen material from the fourteenth series of the show.
Making up Richard Ayoade's self-quizzing collective this time are Dane Baptiste, Angela Barnes, and Lou Sanders.
Monster Mash: The smash-hit quiz show that caught on in a flash. Beastly quizmaster Stephen Fry is an eerie sight for Sara Pascoe, Josh Widdicombe, Phill Jupitus and Alan Davies.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Pub Grub: The bikers head to the pub to explore the story behind the boom in pub food and snacks and recreate mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud.
A man kidnapped by a Mexican drug cartel flees across the bridge into America. At Progreso International Bridge, a woman is caught hiding anti-anxiety pills.
A refugee from Venezuela tells officers how she paid human traffickers to smuggle her to the Mexico-US border. In Laredo, an American couple are caught smuggling methamphetamine.
Documentary series. One man and a huge train take on the unforgiving Pilbara, and four extreme train enthusiasts in Sydney tackle its rail network in the All Stations Challenge.
Bangladesh: Rick Stein ends his epic culinary tour in Bangladesh and, not surprisingly, curry is at the top of his list of local delicacies.
A truck with a shipment of wet wipes turns out to have a secret hidden inside, and a US citizen is caught with thousands of Tramadol tablets hidden in his hand luggage.
A huge shipment of cocaine is found hidden in the floor of a truck, a young man tries to enter the US to enrol in school, and a woman is caught smuggling fentanyl pills.
Mountain Rescue Dog Handler: Guy loves dogs - he therefore jumps at the opportunity to become part of a dog mountain rescue team tackling dangerous terrain as a mountain rescue guide.
Jeremy Clarkson subjects the Skoda Yeti to a thorough road test, while James May visits the test track in the insane Aerial Atom V8.
The lads go head-to-head against their opposite numbers from the Australian Top Gear in a car-based version of The Ashes - howzat sound? Tennis star Boris Becker also guests.
Pub Grub: The bikers head to the pub to explore the story behind the boom in pub food and snacks and recreate mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud.
Bangladesh: Rick Stein ends his epic culinary tour in Bangladesh and, not surprisingly, curry is at the top of his list of local delicacies.
Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell sort fact from fiction with guests John Cooper Clarke, Judi Love, Chris McCausland and Caroline Quentin.
Retired footballer David James, comedians Rhys James and Isy Suttie, and athletics legend Denise Lewis tackle the best-selling author's fun-filled brainteasers in this edition.
More mind-bending puzzles and fun with the ever-genial Pointless personality - he's joined this time by Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams.
Messy: Quite complex quizzing. Things get messy as Stephen Fry learns whether Sarah Millican, Eddie Kadi, Noel Fielding and Alan Davies know their mugwumps from their mullipuffs.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Alexander Armstrong is host as comedians Jo Brand and Munya Chawawa join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
